items.title: It's come to my attention that kids these days are arguing that Michael Jordan did not have a great basketball IQ, and are using LeBron as an example.
items.author: Morten Stig Jensen
items.body_excerpt:
It's come to my attention that kids these days are arguing that Michael Jordan did not have a great basketball IQ, and are using LeBron as an example.

I have no interest the MJ/LBJ debate, but I'm sorry.

Michael Jordan not having great basketball IQ?

Michael. Jordan.

What are we doing here?

items.title: I have the Pelicans as ~12.1M under the luxury tax with 13 players on the roster. This means they can be players using a portion of the full NTMLE either by signing free agent or absorbing a trade.
items.author: Shamit Dua
items.body_excerpt:
I have the Pelicans as ~12.1M under the luxury tax with 13 players on the roster. This means they can be players using a portion of the full NTMLE either by signing free agent or absorbing a trade. 

Or they can do some uneven trades that bring back more salary than sent out

items.author: New York Post

items.title: What a financial career turnaround for Hartenstein.
items.author: Morten Stig Jensen
items.body_excerpt:
What a financial career turnaround for Hartenstein.

First contract: Three years, $3.9 million

Second contract: Two years, $3.3 million

Third contract: One year, $1.7 million

Fourth contract: Two years, $16 million

Thunder: $134 million (expected)
